items.title: 'Additional character' in payroll file leaves North Canberra Hospital staff unpaid

children.content: Health Minister Rachel Stephen-Smith says the government is working to resolve the issue that has left thousands of North Canberra Hospital staff waiting for their fortnightly pay.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: This man plays the last post to a different fallen Anzac every day

children.content: Adam Farquharson feels the honour of Anzac Day every day as he plays to a different veteran's grave.

image.altText: A man in black plays a silver instrument in a graveyard

items.title: 'Concerning': Police reveal DNA links same man to two alleged incidents

children.content: Two separate incidents nearly a year apart in Canberra’s south appeared unrelated until ACT Policing used DNA analysis to link them to the same man.

image.altText: A drawing of a man with dark hair and beard.

items.title: 'Finding hidden gems' in 100 years of soil stored at national archive

children.content: Far from brown dirt, the Australian National Soil Archive houses 100,000 soil samples — some a century old — that represent a slice through time of the ground beneath our feet.

gallery.altText: Three old jars of soil with faded labels.

thumbnail.altText: Three old jars of soil with faded labels.

items.title: Rattenbury denies quitting over coalition talks, but they can't have helped

children.content: In announcing his retirement from ACT politics, Greens leader Shane Rattenbury says he is looking forward to finding out what his next career move could be.

image.altText: A man with short hair wearing a blazer stands outdoors looking serious.

items.title: Educators plead not guilty to assaulting students in Queanbeyan school

children.content: Four educators accused of assaulting children, aged 9 and 10, at a Queanbeyan school were not present in court when their lawyers indicated they would fight the charges.

image.altText: A building with a sign 'Queanbeyan Courthouse'

items.title: 'All you want as a parent': The disability program leading to home ownership

children.content: Not-for-profit Project Independence uses residents' disability support pension to share in a property, giving them legal ownership of their home and allowing them to build equity over time.

gallery.altText: A woman with long dark hair stands smiling outdoors, with a man and woman with grey hair on either side of her.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with long dark hair stands smiling outdoors, with a man and woman with grey hair on either side of her.

items.title: James Valentine swapped microphones — and found a new audience who loved him

children.content: James Valentine started his career on the stage, but it was his time behind the microphone in the studio and an interactive style that came to define him.

articles.title: James Valentine, beloved ABC talkback radio star, dies aged 64

children.content: The presenter and professional saxophone player is being remembered for his ability to explore the humour and oddities of daily life on the airwaves.

image.altText: a man sitting down smiling while clasping his hands together

gallery.altText: a man smiles while holding his chin

thumbnail.altText: a man smiles while holding his chin

items.title: E-7 Wedgetails are world leading, so why are they up for replacement?

children.content: Australia's fleet of world-leading surveillance aircraft is set to be replaced and analysts say the planes could be replaced by drones.

image.altText: A plane flies over a city

items.title: 'Bordering on bullying': Voss hits out at coverage of Hollands

children.content: Michael Voss has called out the media for the way it has covered Elijah Hollands's medical episode, saying the coverage of the 23-year-old "bordered on bullying".

image.altText: Michael Voss and Elijah Hollands stand next to each other

Large container ship on waterways in the Strait of Hormuz

updates.title: Iranian state media shows video of soldiers boarding container ships

items.title: Authors leave publisher after kids book cancelled for illustrator's comments

children.content: Writers including Evelyn Araluen and Randa Abdel-Fattah have left University of Queensland Press after the publisher axed publication of an Indigenous kids book over its illustrator's past 'anti-Semitic' comments.

image.altText: A young woman with cropped hair, wearing large gold earrings and a scarf over her shoulders.

items.title: Actor suing Rebel Wilson agrees bath incident was 'strange and bizarre'

children.content: A young actor suing Rebel Wilson for defamation has given details in court about an incident at Bondi Beach, which she claims the Hollywood star has since mischaracterised.

image.altText: Charlotte MacInnes arriving outside court

items.title: Kingpin's private phone calls break open Mongols' 'culture of silence'

children.content: Detectives say bikies are recruiting inside Northern Territory prisons, as a kingpin's tapped private phone calls offer a rare glimpse into the underground world of outlaw motorcycle gangs.

image.altText: A red white and blue skull embroidered into patches sewn into vests.

items.title: 'Like a slasher movie' as major ASX stock crashes

children.content: Major healthcare stock Cochlear crashed yesterday. Buyers have not moved in today. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Removing plastic from our food chain yields quick results, researchers say

children.content: Perth researchers find levels of plastic chemicals in the body can be reduced within a week by avoiding plastic food packaging and kitchen utensils.

items.title: Stump that created hole in footpath becomes neighbourhood attraction

children.content: Adelaide residents have decorated a large tree stump and then the hole it left in a footpath to highlight the local council's delay in removing it.

image.altText: A rotten tree stump surrounded by traffic cones, including one topped by a green hat

items.title: Small WA town takes centre stage for Netflix series filming

children.content: There is a buzz about town in a small community north of Perth as filming begins for a new Netflix series.

image.altText: A film crew in front of beige buildings.

items.title: Leading Trump envoy suggests FIFA replace Iran with Italy at World Cup

children.content: As the US president feuds with Pope Leo, one of his top envoys suggests giving Italy a World Cup spot could mend fences.

image.altText: Italy's men's football team poses for a photo

items.title: Women on mission to preserve history of town's soldier settlers

children.content: Anzac Day holds meaning for every Australian, but for Marie Carman and Lorelei King, it is another reminder of their deeply personal mission to uncover the forgotten stories of the soldiers who helped build their town.

image.altText: Two older women smile together out the front of the El Arish Diggers Museum.

items.title: Police officer given 'golden pass' on rape allegations, victim says

children.content: When Tasmania Police misidentified Laura as a family violence perpetrator, she had no idea she'd have to fight for them to hold her abuser — a serving officer — to account. Now, she's fighting for change.

gallery.altText: A police officer's torso showing a body cam

thumbnail.altText: A police officer's torso showing a body cam

items.title: New AI threat looms but Australian firms don't have access needed to prepare

children.content: Local banks, power providers and infrastructure firms do not have access to test their systems against a powerful new AI cybersecurity risk, Anthropic's Mythos, which has not been made public.

image.altText: Anthropic logo on a smartphone screen

items.title: Pacific leaders 'totally blindsided' by declaration of fuel emergency

children.content: Confusion and some frustration remains after a key Pacific leader announced an emergency declaration had been agreed to over fuel, despite other leaders not knowing about it.

image.altText: A man in a suit rests his hand on his chest, standing in front of Solomon Island and China flags.

items.title: 'Not a great look': Piastri weighs in on Verstappen's F1 frustrations

children.content: Australian star Oscar Piastri speaks as Formula 1 champion Max Verstappen looks increasingly likely to walk away from the sport.

image.altText: An image of three F1 drivers speaking during an interview in pit lane.

items.title: Farmers say an easy solution to the mouse plague has been rejected

children.content: Farmers are pushing for stronger bait approval as mice numbers soar out of control in Western Australia, but stronger poisons
are being rejected by the national chemical regulator.

image.altText: Feral mice in a bucket.

items.title: No more green bin pick-ups for some residents amid fuel uncertainty

children.content: The Upper Lachlan Shire Council says it cannot afford to be caught off-guard if a diesel shipment does not arrive, and must reserve fuel for essential services like road grading or red bin collections.

gallery.altText: Council bins with green lids on suburban street.

thumbnail.altText: Council bins with green lids on suburban street.

items.title: 'Not a nice place to live at the moment': Youth crime leaving Kununurra at wits' end

children.content: Stolen cars and accommodation break-ins have led to growing frustration in Western Australia's north.

image.altText: A couple stand looking sternly with a car behind them

items.title: A dozen new offences added to LNP's adult crime, adult time laws in Qld

children.content: Conspiring to commit murder, unlawful stalking and riot are among 12 new offences that juveniles can now be sentenced as adults for.

image.altText: Large billboard of man with politcal slogans

items.title: Coroner refers Byron death of Melbourne van-packer to homicide police

children.content: The NSW state coroner recommends the death of van-packer Jackson Stacker near Byron Bay be referred to the Unsolved Homicide Squad.

items.title: Thousands sign petition against McDonald's Phillip Island restaurant plan

children.content: The fast food giant is facing backlash after lodging a planning permit application to build a new restaurant in tourist hotspot Cowes.

items.title: Tears for Ugle-Hagan as ex-Bulldog prepares to make Suns debut

children.content: Jamarra Ugle-Hagan endured a year from hell in 2025, but things are looking up for him this season as he prepares to make his Gold Coast debut.

items.title: Alleged drink driver detected at 151km/h with child in ute, police say

children.content: A man who was allegedly drink driving with a child inside a speeding ute in Adelaide's south last night will face court after being stripped of his licence, police say.

image.altText: An SA Police logo.

items.title: 15yo boy dies in collision involving e-motorbike in Melbourne's north

children.content: A teenager died after being thrown from an unregistered electric motorcycle at South Morang on Wednesday evening.

image.altText: Two police officers standing near a bike.

items.title: 50 years of Ramones — the album nobody bought but everyone copied

children.content: Fed up with where rock 'n' roll was headed, these four New Yorkers changed the music world by playing sweet pop hooks at breakneck speed.
